design_identity.name: Organic Wealth & Strategic Clarity
design_identity.description: A fusion of Swiss Design precision (for data) and Organic warmth (for human connection). It avoids the sterile 'Blue Bank' aesthetic by using deep forest greens, bone whites, and terracotta accents.
design_identity.mood: Professional, grounded, yet optimistic. Like a high-end architectural firm managing your finances.
design_identity.theme_strategy: Light Mode default (Trust/Paper feel). Dark Mode available (Premium/Night Owl).

headings.usage: Use for all H1-H4. It has a slight 'technical' quirk that fits the simulator engine.

body.usage: UI elements, long text, data tables.

data_display.usage: For financial figures in the simulator to ensure alignment.

marketing.name: The Bento/Tetris Grid
rules: Never fill 100% perfectly; leave negative space

dashboard.name: High Density Data Grid
rules: Stretch height (h-full) for all cards in a row

buttons.primary: rounded-full px-8 py-3 bg-emerald-900 text-white hover:bg-emerald-800 transition-transform active:scale-95 shadow-lg
buttons.secondary: rounded-full px-8 py-3 border border-stone-300 text-stone-700 hover:bg-stone-100 transition-colors

cards.style: bg-white dark:bg-stone-900 border border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800 rounded-xl shadow-sm hover:shadow-md transition-shadow duration-300

inputs.style: h-12 rounded-lg border-stone-300 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-emerald-900/20 focus:border-emerald-900 bg-white dark:bg-stone-900

visual_enhancers.noise: opacity-5 mix-blend-overlay pointer-events-none absolute inset-0 bg-[url('/noise.png')]

hero_section.alt: Modern minimalist home representing the dream goal

growth_feature.alt: Small plant growing, symbolizing savings and SIP

planning_feature.alt: Person working on laptop in a cafe
planning_feature.usage: Feature card for 'EMI Simulator' - planning freedom

success_feature.usage: Call to Action section or Success state

accessibility.contrast_check: Ensure text-stone-600 is legible on bg-stone-100

instructions: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur)
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯📚 for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...)
Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...})

testing.rule: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute
